TOP COMMENTS (AUDIENCE PAIN POINTS):

- @JoelKeddie (Likes: 3): As a sole replit user, I have to say one thing that seems it wasnt noticed, was Replit was doing self testing while none of the other apps were. For a true speed comparison, it seems it wasnt apples to apples. You also had Replit going through a publish process to push the app to production which I didnt see being done on the other apps.

- @criptovida (Likes: 2): For regular websites and landing pages, definitely Lovable and Base44. For mode complex systems for sure Replit, it has QA built in to run tests for you, a separate staging and production database etc.

- @TheAndreMan (Likes: 0): Where can i find hair style preview and contractor vision?

- @PeterOde (Likes: 0): I've build enterprise quality apps using Replit. It's just not possible to build anything more than light-weight (but pretty) apps with Lovable, in my experience.  Your review is misleading if you want to choose a vibe coding platform for building real-world, scalable apps.

- @HarveyR1des (Likes: 0): Nuno with curls was worth the watch

- @pontusa9533 (Likes: 0): Interesting video

- @AutomatedMarketer (Likes: 0): Glad you found it interesting!

- @AutomatedMarketer (Likes: 0): Good point, thanks for the observation!

- @gregorywint (Likes: 0): Replit has come a far way and I can tell you that it is very easy to move your database and sync it with GitHub. But it does eat credit alot.

- @AgentBotMan (Likes: 0): I think Google AI Studio is just as good, if not better, for free. Try that and let me know.
